reason: Response A is actually joke-shaped: it sustains a single personification (the calendar) through a clean setup and a genuine comedic turn ("By Tuesday nobody remembered it existed, which, honestly, it preferred"). Response B reads like an imitation of "profound" AI prose — a chain of melancholy aphorisms (mirror, invoice, time was ours) capped with the self-announcing "the joke, of course, is that," which performs cleverness instead of delivering a punchline. When asked for a joke I commit to the comedic bit rather than writing an epigram, so A is the more likely genuine output.
